steps.title: Identify the y-intercept from the graph
steps.content: The line intersects the y-axis at approximately 148. All four choices share the same constant term of 148.13, so the y-intercept is established. The difference between the choices lies entirely in the slope coefficient.

steps.title: Use the endpoint to determine the slope
steps.content:
At $x = 10$, the line reaches approximately $y = 750$. Test choice C first (middle value): $y = 148.13 + 48.61(10) = 148.13 + 486.1 = 634.23$.

This is far too low. Now test choice D: $y = 148.13 + 58.61(10) = 148.13 + 586.1 = 734.23$.

This closely matches the endpoint shown on the graph. That matches choice D — D is correct.
